title: Mark Zuckerberg launches an AI-optimism campaign, and a jab at the doomers
excerpt: The AI industry has a mood problem, and Mark Zuckerberg thinks he can sell the cure. On Thursday, Meta’s chief executive posted a sunny vision of the AI future, and wrapped it in a marketing campaign. In a Facebook post, Zuckerberg said Meta “has always believed in giving people the power to share, connect, and […]
content_html:
The AI industry has a mood problem, and Mark Zuckerberg thinks he can sell the cure. On Thursday, Meta’s chief executive posted a sunny vision of the AI future, and wrapped it in a marketing campaign.

In a Facebook post, Zuckerberg said Meta “has always believed in giving people the power to share, connect, and shape your world,” Axios reported.

The video ad that came with it was blunter. “Some people will have you believe AI will make us less connected … We couldn’t disagree more,” it says. “Call us optimists, call us dreamers … we’re betting on people.”

The interesting words there are “some people.” Zuckerberg never names them. He does not have to.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

The ad is a not-so-subtle shot at Meta’s rivals. Anthropic and OpenAI have leaned into warnings, about jobs vanishing and about AI-driven cyberattacks. Zuckerberg’s counter is relentless positivity.

“22 years and 3.5 billion people later, we’re doubling down,” the ad says. “The future is for everyone.” A source confirmed to Axios that it forms part of a broader paid and earned media campaign.

There is strategy under the sentiment. While several AI giants chase enterprise customers, Meta is staking out the opposite ground: free, mass-market, for everyone. It is the same logic that built Facebook, now pointed at the agentic future.

The optimism gap

Zuckerberg is not alone in pushing back on the doom. Nvidia’s Jensen Huang has called the darkest AI predictions “complete nonsense,” and Adecco’s boss argues AI is changing jobs, not destroying them. The difference is that Zuckerberg has turned the argument into advertising.

The irony is not lost on anyone.

Meta spent the social-media era apologising for technology’s effects on society, from elections to teenagers. Now it wants to be the optimistic face of the next wave. Whether the public buys a campaign about trust from a company still fighting child-safety lawsuits is the real test.

The future may be for everyone. The question is who they will trust to build it.

Published July 23, 2026 - 2:53 pm UTC

Back to top

sentences.zh: 发布时间 UTC 时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 下午 2:53

content_html_zh:
人工智能行业存在情绪问题，马克·扎克伯格认为他可以推销治愈方法。 周四，Meta 的首席执行官发布了对人工智能未来的乐观愿景，并将其纳入营销活动中。

据 Axios 报道，扎克伯格在 Facebook 帖子中表示，Meta“始终相信赋予人们分享、连接和塑造世界的能力”。

随之而来的视频广告则更加直白。 “有些人会让你相信人工智能会让我们的联系减少…… 我们完全不同意，”它说。 “称我们为乐观主义者，称我们为梦想家...... 我们把赌注押在人身上。”

有趣的词是“有些人”。 扎克伯格从未说出他们的名字。 他不必这样做。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

这则广告毫不含蓄地针对 Meta 的竞争对手。 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 都倾向于对工作岗位消失和人工智能驱动的网络攻击发出警告。 扎克伯格的应对措施是坚持不懈的积极性。

广告中写道：“22 年后，我们将加倍努力，为 35 亿人口服务。” “未来属于每个人。” 一位消息人士向 Axios 证实，这是更广泛的付费和免费媒体活动的一部分。

情怀之下有策略。 虽然几家人工智能巨头都在追逐企业客户，但 Meta 却采取了相反的立场：免费、面向大众市场、面向所有人。 建立 Facebook 的逻辑与现在指向代理未来的逻辑相同。

乐观差距

扎克伯格并不是唯一一个反抗这一厄运的人。 英伟达的黄仁勋称最黑暗的人工智能预测“完全是无稽之谈”，而阿德科的老板则认为人工智能正在改变工作，而不是摧毁它们。 不同的是，扎克伯格把争论变成了广告。

任何人都不会忽视其中的讽刺意义。

在社交媒体时代，Meta 一直在为技术对社会（从选举到青少年）的影响道歉。 现在它想成为下一波浪潮的乐观面孔。 公众是否会接受一家仍在与儿童安全诉讼作斗争的公司发起的关于信任的宣传活动，才是真正的考验。

未来可能属于每个人。 问题是他们将信任谁来建造它。

发布时间 UTC 时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 下午 2:53

Back to top
